summary: Early Italian Renaissance painter from Florence. Known for The Tribute Money, Expulsion from the Garden of Eden, and The Holy Trinity — all in Florence. Pioneered linear perspective (learned from Brunelleschi). Student of Masolino. His 'clumsy/messy' nickname contrasts with Masolino's diminutive nickname.

works.name: The Tribute Money (Brancacci Chapel)
works.description: Fresco in the Brancacci Chapel at Santa Maria del Carmine, Florence. Shows Christ directing Peter to retrieve a coin from a fish's mouth to pay a tax collector; the tax collector appears twice. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Jesus in blue robe directs St. Peter to retrieve a coin from a fish's mouth to give to a tax collector; the tax collector appears twice
examples: depicts Christ directing Peter to retrieve a coin from a fish's mouth
a tax collector appears twice after St. Peter fetches money from the mouth of a fish

works.name: Expulsion from the Garden of Eden (Brancacci Chapel)
works.description: Fresco of a sword-wielding angel expelling a weeping Adam and Eve from Eden. Cosimo III ordered fig leaves added to cover genitalia. Wikipedia

images.caption: Expulsion from the Garden of Eden

clues.clue: Sword-wielding angel expels weeping Adam and Eve from Eden; Cosimo III ordered fig leaves added
examples: a weeping Adam and Eve being expelled from Eden
Cosimo III de' Medici ordered that fig leaves cover the genitalia of two figures being led away by a sword-wielding angel

works.name: The Holy Trinity (Santa Maria Novella)
works.description: Fresco in Santa Maria Novella, Florence. Uses linear perspective for a barrel vault. God holds the crucified Christ above a skeleton with the inscription 'I was once what you are, and what I am you will become.' Hidden by Vasari, reconstructed in 1952. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Barrel vault fresco using linear perspective learned from Brunelleschi; crucifixion above a skeleton with inscription 'I was once what you are'
examples: used a nail and strings at the foot of the cross for the vanishing point perspective of a barrel vault
A skeleton lies just below a Latin phrase which translates as 'I once was what you are and what I am you also will be'

clues.clue: Florentine Early Renaissance painter; student of Masolino
examples: this Italian master whose Brancacci Chapel works include...

clues.clue: Nickname means 'clumsy/messy'; contrasts with Masolino's diminutive nickname
examples: teacher had a diminutive nickname to contrast with his own 'clumsy' or 'messy' nickname
Linear perspective was pioneered in this city's Holy Trinity fresco by the native artist Masaccio

comprehensive_summary:
Masaccio (Tommaso di Ser Giovanni di Simone, 1401-1428) was an early Italian Renaissance painter who pioneered linear perspective. His nickname means 'clumsy' or 'messy,' contrasting with his teacher Masolino's diminutive nickname.

His most famous works are in the Brancacci Chapel at Santa Maria del Carmine in Florence. The Tribute Money shows Jesus in a blue robe directing Peter to retrieve a coin from a fish's mouth to pay a tax collector, who appears twice in the composition. The Expulsion from the Garden of Eden depicts a sword-wielding angel expelling weeping Adam and Eve; Cosimo III ordered fig leaves added to cover their genitalia.

The Holy Trinity at Santa Maria Novella uses linear perspective (learned from Brunelleschi) for a barrel vault, with God holding the crucified Christ above a skeleton bearing the inscription 'I was once what you are, and what I am you will become.' It was hidden by Vasari and reconstructed in 1952.
